title: alnus mandschurica f. pubescens
intro: Alnus mandschurica f. pubescens is a variant of the Manchurian alder tree, characterized by its hairy or pubescent leaves, which are a distinctive feature of this subspecies. The bark and leaves of Alnus mandschurica f. pubescens have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic. Alnus mandschurica f. pubescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in herbal remedies and supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
